page: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel Xeon 8176M processors running at 2.1 GHz. The system delivers 448 dedicated vCPUs across 224 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 13.71 GB of RAM per physical core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but features a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network interface.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 18894.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 810.78 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Redis SET operations perform at a top-tier rate of 297.29 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 100.48 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more modest, with Geekbench single-core scores and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the lower percentiles. LLM text generation speeds are also weak, reflecting the older CPU generation and lack of hardware accelerators.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a specialized, high-density hardware class designed to consolidate massive in-memory databases. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. The u-6tb1.112xlarge is highly suited for large-scale enterprise workloads such as SAP HANA, massive in-memory databases, real-time data analytics, and heavy caching layers like Redis, where memory capacity and high-throughput networking are critical.
description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for massive in-memory workloads. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features an x86_64 architecture with Intel Xeon 8176M processors, providing 448 dedicated vCPUs across 224 physical cores. It is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 100 Gbps baseline network speed, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with cached reads exceeding 18,000 GB/sec and Redis SET operations reaching 297.29 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation are relatively weak. This instance is highly optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications like SAP HANA, and high-throughput caching layers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 448 vCPUs, 6144 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge features 448 vCPUs, 6 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking for large-scale in-memory databases.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized platform delivering six terabytes of RAM and high-throughput networking for enterprise in-memory databases.
bullet_points: Equipped with 6144 GB of DDR4 memory and 448 dedicated vCPUs
Features a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network interface
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators
Best suited for massive in-memory databases and SAP HANA
